steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Cross-Text Connections question. Both texts are about choreographer Alvin Ailey. The question asks what both authors would most likely agree on. The answer must be supported by evidence in both texts — not just one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Dancers who worked with Ailey greatly appreciated his supportive approach as a choreographer.': Neither text mentions how Ailey's dancers felt about his approach. Text 2 describes his method but says nothing about dancers' appreciation. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Ailey's work was strongly influenced by jazz.': Text 1 states Ailey had a "deep admiration for jazz music" felt in the rhythms and beats of his works. Text 2 states "Jazz is present throughout Ailey's work" and explains how jazz principles shaped his choreography. Both authors agree jazz was a strong influence. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Audiences were mostly unfamiliar with the jazz music in Ailey's works.': Neither text addresses audience familiarity with jazz. Text 1 says Ailey "helped bring jazz to life for his audiences," but that does not imply audiences were unfamiliar with it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Ailey blended multiple genres of music together when choreographing dance pieces.': Neither text mentions any genre other than jazz. Introducing "multiple genres" is unsupported by either passage. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
